title: YOLO v4 to v5
concept: YOLOv4 and v5 introduced CSP backbones and training techniques like Mosaic Augmentation to boost accuracy without slowing down inference.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What are anchor boxes in YOLOv2/v3?

options.text: Predefined bounding box shapes used as templates for predictions
Weights that anchor the model during backward passes
A way to crop the edges of the image

exercises.explain: Correct! Anchor boxes provide preset shapes to help the model predict coordinates more easily.

cards.title: The Augmentation Hack
cards.body: What if you could make an object detector much more accurate without changing the network's speed at all? In 2020, YOLOv4 and YOLOv5 did exactly this by introducing clever training tricks, including 'Mosaic Data Augmentation'.

cards.title: YOLO v4 & v5 Enhancements
cards.body: YOLOv4 introduced CSPDarknet (Cross-Stage Partial networks) to optimize information flow, and 'Bag of Freebies' like Mosaic Augmentation. YOLOv5 moved the architecture into PyTorch, making it extremely easy to train, deploy, and auto-calculate anchor boxes.

cards.title: The Photo Collage
cards.body: Mosaic data augmentation is like making a collage by cutting four different photos and pasting them together into one. It forces the model to look at objects in unusual contexts and scales, making it much more robust.

cards.title: Mosaic Training
cards.body: During training, YOLOv5 cuts four images (a street, a park, a cat, a beach) and merges them into one grid. The model learns to detect the car from the street even when it's next to the cat, simulating complex backgrounds.

cards.title: Who Made YOLOv5?
cards.body: A common myth is that YOLOv5 was published by the original YOLO creator. Joseph Redmon actually stopped AI research due to ethical concerns. YOLOv5 was developed by Glenn Jocher, transitioning YOLO into the PyTorch ecosystem.

left.text:
1 image scaled/flipped
Slight variety in coordinates
Struggles with small contexts

right.text:
4 images stitched into one
Forces model to see small objects
Simulates high-density scenes

exercises.prompt: How does Mosaic Data Augmentation work during YOLO training?

options.text: It stitches four different training images into a single training input
It blurs the image to simulate motion
It converts bounding boxes into a pixelated mosaic filter
It automatically labels unlabeled images online

exercises.explain: Correct! Mosaic augmentation stitches four images together, creating high density and rich contexts.

exercises.prompt: True or False: YOLOv5 was written in PyTorch, which made it much easier for developers to integrate and train compared to the older C-based Darknet framework.
exercises.explain: True. Moving to PyTorch democratized YOLO, leading to massive adoption in industry.

exercises.sentence: YOLOv4 introduced the CSPDarknet backbone, where CSP stands for Cross-Stage ___.
exercises.explain: CSP stands for Cross-Stage Partial, which splits gradient flows to save memory and increase speed.

exercises.prompt: What is a 'Bag of Freebies' in YOLO training?

options.text: Training techniques that increase accuracy without increasing inference time
Free cloud GPUs provided by researchers

exercises.explain: Correct! 'Bag of Freebies' includes augmentations and loss tricks that only affect training time, leaving inference speed untouched.

exercises.prompt: You are training a YOLOv5 model to detect tiny defects on microchips, but you have very few training images. Which technique is most likely to help the model learn to find these tiny defects?

options.text: Mosaic Augmentation, because it naturally shrinks objects and mixes their backgrounds, helping the model generalize to tiny objects.
Disabling all data augmentation to keep images clean.
Using a larger grid cell size to merge pixels.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Mosaic augmentation is incredibly powerful for training models to find small objects in limited datasets.
